options.content:
**Tiered Claude sampling.** Each default-lane request rolls a die. Probability of trying Claude depends on how much we've spent today: **10% up to $40, then 1% up to $60, 0.1% up to $80** — each $20 of spend cuts the sample rate by 10×. Ollama handles the rest. No hard cap, no wall — Claude usage just becomes exponentially rare as spend climbs. Self-converges around $41/day even without a cutoff. Interactive web sessions bypass the dice entirely and always get Claude.

**Ollama model pool.** 2–4 concurrent models run on the 5090 (.240). Each handles up to **100 requests** then drains gracefully — no new conversations, finishes its existing ones — and goes back of the queue. New conversations stick to a random loaded model for their entire lifetime. The scheduler watches VRAM, GPU util, and TTFT every 10s; it grows the pool only when there's comfortable headroom (50%+ VRAM free, ≤60% GPU util) so other workloads on .240 stay healthy. Fast models naturally cycle more often.

panels.title: 🟢 Right now — utilization across both providers

panels.description: 2m rolling rate of requests the gateway admitted to Ollama (in-flight < NUM_PARALLEL).
panels.title: Ollama admits/sec

panels.description: 2m rolling rate of requests the gateway admitted to Claude on the default lane (spend < $20 AND under linear pace).
panels.title: Claude admits/sec

panels.description: Concurrent Ollama requests on this gateway pod. Hits the OLLAMA_SATURATION_THRESHOLD (8 by default) → spillover fires.
panels.title: Ollama in-flight (live)

panels.description: Admission denials projected to per-hour rate. With probabilistic Claude sampling, denials come from Ollama saturation (reason=ollama_saturated). claude_sanity_sentinel is the $200 paranoia ceiling and should never fire.
panels.title: Ollama-saturation backoffs / hour
💸 Self-throttling pulse — Claude spend + caller waits

panels.description: Combined Claude spend today: default-lane (subject to $20 cap) + interactive lane (uncapped).
panels.title: Claude spent today (default + interactive)

panels.description: Probability the next default-lane request will try Claude. Decays 10× at each $20 above $40.
panels.title: Current Claude sample rate

panels.description: Caller backoffs (429 Retry-After) per 5-minute window. The system's pulse — both heroes (Ollama in-flight + Callers waiting) read 'is the system self-throttling correctly?'
panels.title: Callers waiting / 5m
🌊 Calm beach wave — admitted req/s by provider

panels.description: Admitted request rate by provider. Both lines should ride together as the dual-lane router balances load.
panels.title: Calm beach wave — admitted req/s by provider

panels.description: Live in-flight count averaged across gateway pods. When it crosses 8 (the OLLAMA_SATURATION_THRESHOLD), the dispatcher starts spilling.
panels.title: Ollama in-flight vs threshold

panels.description: Per-minute rate of admission denials by reason. Stacked bars: cyan=ollama_saturated, orange=claude_overpace, red=claude_daily_cap_reached, magenta=both.
panels.title: Admission denials by reason
💰 Claude capacity pacing — default lane vs interactive lane vs ceiling

panels.description: Default-lane spend (green, filled) and interactive-lane spend (cyan→orange→red as it crosses $5/$20). Horizontal lines mark the sampling-tier boundaries at $40 / $60 / $80 / $100. Background tint shades each tier.
panels.title: Claude spend by lane — tier boundaries shown

panels.description: Full-width status banner driven by the interactive-lane spend. Green = normal. Orange = elevated ($5+). Red = runaway ($20+).

result.text: 🚨 Interactive lane runaway — burning faster than the entire default budget

panels.title: Interactive lane status

panels.description: Interactive-lane spend today (uncapped). Background turns yellow at $5, red at $20.
panels.title: Interactive lane spend today
🔄 Ollama rotation — one model per 5-min window

panels.description: Per-model request rate. With prewarm + OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=2, each window's model takes over hot.
panels.title: Active model — rate by gen_ai.request.model (Ollama only)

panels.description: nc-chatbot, sb-router, nc-gift-finder, etc. — the specialists doing the actual chatting.
panels.title: Request rate by ai_o11y.specialist

panels.description: histogram_quantile p95 over the gen_ai_client_operation_duration_seconds histogram.
panels.title: Provider p95 latency

panels.description: Target min 2, max 4. Adapts to VRAM availability.
panels.title: Pool size (live)

panels.description: What the scheduler decided this cycle: add capacity, hold steady, or drain a model.
panels.title: Scheduler verdict

panels.description: Percent of 5090 VRAM in use. Drain trigger fires above 70%.
panels.title: GPU VRAM

panels.description: Live GPU compute utilization on .240. Same thresholds as VRAM.
panels.title: GPU util (5m)

panels.description: Each model's request count climbing toward 100, then resetting to 0 on drain. One line per model. Lines that flatten near 100 are about to be drained.
panels.title: Per-model lifecycle progress — requests served toward 100

panels.description: Each row is one model. Cell color: cyan=loading, green=active, orange=draining.
panels.title: Model state timeline

panels.description: Active conversations pinned to each loaded model. Sessions stay on one model for their entire lifetime.
panels.title: Sticky session count per model

panels.description: How many models are queued waiting for VRAM headroom. High queue = pool is operating below its potential due to GPU constraints.
panels.title: Queue depth (models waiting their turn)

panels.description: All requests specialists fire at the LLM gateway (admitted + denied).
panels.title: Submissions / min

panels.description: Share of submissions the gateway lets through to a provider.
panels.title: Admitted %

panels.description: Share that the gateway rejects (saturation, sampling, budget).
panels.title: Denied %

panels.description: Distinct specialists with at least one gateway call in the last 5 minutes.
panels.title: Active specialists / 5m

panels.description: What happens to every request: admit-ollama (green) + admit-anthropic (blue) ride above the line; deny-ollama (red) is the rejection band.
panels.title: Submissions / min — stacked by decision × provider

panels.description: How long callers sit in the gateway's queue before they get a verdict. Climbing tail = saturation building up.
panels.title: Caller wait — p50 / p95 / p99

panels.description: Why the gateway said no, broken out by reason label.
panels.title: Denial reasons / min — stacked

panels.description: Every specialist that fires requests, plotted over time. Sourced from gen_ai_client_operation_duration_seconds_count (recorded by the specialist when it talks to the gateway).
panels.title: Per-specialist gateway calls / min — time series

panels.description: Sortable table of every active specialist's call rate. NOTE: gateway admit/deny is not labeled per-specialist, so this is the attempted call rate from each caller (not the admitted rate).
panels.title: Per-specialist gateway calls — leaderboard

title: AI o11y — Loadgen Activity (provider capacity router)
